---
title: "nitrix.geometry.topology"
generated: true
signature-hash: 1da4ac4316c434ec
description: "Mesh topology invariants -- the genus-0 defect gate."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-topology-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-topology/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.topology`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Mesh topology invariants -- the genus-0 defect gate.

Cheap combinatorial invariants of a triangle mesh: the Euler characteristic
$\chi = V - E + F$ and (for a closed orientable surface) the genus
$g = (2 - \chi) / 2$.  These form a **defect gate** for the field-to-mesh
route: a consumer checks `euler_characteristic(mesh) == 2` (equivalently
[`genus`](nitrix-geometry-topology/genus.qmd) `== 0`) to know whether a marching-cubes / level-set extraction
produced the expected spherical topology -- i.e. whether the template escape
hatch is safe, or a topology correction is required.

These are host-side integer combinatorics; they return Python `int`\ s, not
traced arrays, and are not differentiable.

A full topology *corrector* (in the manner of FreeSurfer's `mris_fix_topology`)
is deliberately out of scope here; the field-to-mesh pipeline keeps the seam open
so that a corrector can slot between extraction and inflation later.

Because there is no corrector yet, raw [`marching_cubes`](nitrix-geometry-isosurface/marching_cubes.qmd) output is
gate-only: if [`genus`](nitrix-geometry-topology/genus.qmd) reports a value `> 0` the topology defect can be
detected but not repaired.  The supported route to a genus-0 surface is a
template-correspondence model followed by [`deform_to_sdf`](nitrix-geometry-surface/deform_to_sdf.qmd), which inherits
genus-0 from the template; a marching-cubes mesh with handles must be corrected
by an external tool.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-topology-symbols}
:::
